questions.questionText: Which fungal organism is dimorphic, presenting as a mold in the cold (environment) and a yeast in the heat (tissues)?
questions.explanation: 'Mold in the cold, Yeast in the beast' applies to Histoplasma, Blastomyces, Coccidioides, etc.

questions.questionText: A heavily encapsulated yeast identified using an India ink preparation of CSF, typically causing meningitis in AIDS patients, is:
questions.explanation: C. neoformans is found in pigeon droppings and has a very thick polysaccharide capsule.

questions.questionText: Rose gardener's disease, presenting as local pustules extending along lymphatic drainage tracks, is caused by:
questions.explanation: Sporothrix is a subcutaneous dimorphic fungus introduced by thorny plant trauma.

questions.questionText: Which ubiquitous mold creates a 'fungus ball' (aspergilloma) inside pre-existing lung cavities (e.g., from old TB)?
questions.explanation: Aspergillus fungi branch at acute (45 degree) angles and are highly prevalent in the environment.

questions.questionText: The most severe form of malaria, causing cerebral malaria and exhibiting 'banana-shaped' gametocytes on a blood smear, is caused by:
questions.explanation: P. falciparum is the most deadly and does not have a dormant liver phase (hypnozoite).

questions.questionText: Which protozoan causes a sexually transmitted infection leading to a frothy, foul-smelling, greenish vaginal discharge and 'strawberry cervix'?
questions.explanation: T. vaginalis is a flagellated, motile trophozoite without a cyst stage.

questions.questionText: Chagas disease (American trypanosomiasis), which causes megacolon, megaesophagus, and cardiomyopathy, is caused by:
questions.explanation: T. cruzi is transmitted by the reduviid ('kissing') bug in the Americas.

questions.questionText: Infection with which helminth (obtained by ingesting undercooked pork/bear meat) causes severe muscle pain and periorbital edema as larvae encyst in striated muscle?
questions.explanation: Trichinosis classically presents with intense myalgia, fever, and extreme eosinophilia.

questions.questionText: The 'Scotch tape test' is used to identify the characteristic eggs of which common intestinal nematode that causes intense perianal pruritus in children?
questions.explanation: Pinworms emerge at night to lay eggs on the perianal folds.

questions.questionText: Hydatid cyst disease in the liver, which can cause fatal anaphylaxis if the cysts rupture, is caused by the dog tapeworm:
questions.explanation: Humans are accidental intermediate hosts upon ingestion of eggs from canine feces.

questions.questionText: In Mycology and Parasitology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Microbiology texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Microbiology references for Mycology and Parasitology. Subject: Microbiology. Chapter: Mycology and Parasitology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Mycology and Parasitology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Microbiology. Chapter: Mycology and Parasitology.
